summary: Merged first-pass migration of the `ft` subsystem to C. The local PR slice has no body, comments, review feedback, changed-file list, or diff excerpt; the stored diff fetch failed because GitHub reported the PR diff exceeded the 300-file limit. Evidence is therefore limited mostly to the PR title and metadata.
what_changed: Began migrating the `ft` area to C, explicitly labeled as "pass 1" in the PR title.

The exact source/header files changed are not available in this slice; changed_files is empty and diff.diff contains only an error indicating the pull request diff exceeded GitHub's maximum file count for that endpoint.
smart_moves: Framed the work as a staged migration pass, which is a useful way to land broad subsystem conversion work incrementally rather than attempting a single all-or-nothing cleanup.
Kept the PR title scoped to the subsystem prefix `ft`, making the intent searchable even though the detailed file list is unavailable in this dump.
decomp_lessons: For very large decompilation migrations, preserve a file manifest or split follow-up knowledge records by subsystem/file group; GitHub diff retrieval can fail once a PR exceeds 300 files.
When a migration is labeled as a numbered pass, future work should look for subsequent passes before assuming the subsystem conversion was complete.
With no review comments or body text available, do not infer matching tactics, struct discoveries, or naming rationale beyond the title-level evidence.
naming_conventions: Uses the project subsystem prefix `ft` to refer to the fighter-related code area.
The phrase "pass 1" signals an incremental migration convention for large decompilation efforts.
searchable_terms: diff exceeded maximum number of files
follow_up_queries: Find subsequent PRs with titles containing `ft`, `Migrate`, or `pass 2` to reconstruct the full fighter migration sequence.
Recover the changed-file list from Git history around merge commit dedebe874350fadddc7c5e09326c44afbc7ce050 because this PR slice lacks changed_files and the diff was too large.
Search for repository changes near 2022-11-02 touching `ft` paths to identify specific files converted to C.
